sections.content:
Now I can see clearly. Comparing the before and after:
BEFORE (row 30-32):
```
30: gwwwwwwwwwNNNwwwwwwNNNwwwppppppwwwwwcccccwww
31: gwwwwwwwwwNWNwwwwwwNBNwwwppppppwwwwwwwwwwwww
32: gwwcccccwwNNNwwwwwwNNNwwwppppppwwwwwwwwwwwww
```
AFTER (row 30-32):
```
30: gwwwwwwwwwwwwNNNwwwNNNwwwppppppwwwwwcccccwww
31: gwwwwwwwwwwwwNWNwwwNBNwwwppppppwwwwwwwwwwwww
32: gwwcccccwwwwwNNNwwwNNNwwwppppppwwwwwwwwwwwww
```
So the green-outlined squares (NNN/NWN/NNN and NNN/NBN/NNN) shifted RIGHT by 3 columns. The white 3x3 block between them also shifted right.
Before: green at cols 18-20, white block at cols 24-26, green at cols 27-29
After: green at cols 21-23, white block at cols 24-26, green at cols 27-29
Wait, let me re-examine. The first green outline was at cols 18-20 and now is at cols 21-23 (moved right 3). The second green outline was at cols 27-29 and is still at cols 27-29. The white block was at cols 30-32 and is now at cols 24-26.
Hmm, that's confusing. Let me look at the actual columns more carefully.
Before:
Row 30: ...wwwNNNwwwwwwNNNwww...
         cols 18-20: NNN
         cols 27-29: NNN
         cols 30-32: www (white block)
After:
Row 30: ...wwwwwNNNwwwNNNwww...
         cols 21-23: NNN
         cols 24-26: www
         cols 27-29: NNN
So it seems like the left green square (with white center) moved right by 3, and the white block that was at cols 30-32 moved left to cols 24-26. They swapped positions somehow.
Wait, actually, I think what happened is:
- The green square with the BLACK center (originally at cols 27-29) is now at cols 27-29 (didn't move)
- The green square with the WHITE center (originally at cols 18-20) moved right by 3 to cols 21-23
- The white block (originally at cols 30-32) moved to cols 24-26
Actually, let me look at the centers. The black pixel was at [31, 28] and is now at [31, 28] (from the segmentation). Wait no, the segmentation said the black pixel id=11 is at [31, 28]. Let me re-read.
After:
- Green outline id=6 at [[30,21], [30,23], [32,23], [32,21]] with child black pixel id=10
- Green outline id=8 at [[30,27], [30,29], [32,29], [32,27]] with child id=11
But the black pixel id=11 is at [31, 28]. So the green outline at cols 27-29 contains the black pixel at col 28. And the green outline at cols 21-23 also contains the black pixel id=10 at... hmm.
Wait, I'm confused about the IDs. Let me re-read:
After:
- Green id=6: boundary [[30,21], [30,23], [32,23], [32,21]], children=[10]
- Green id=8: boundary [[30,27], [30,29], [32,29], [32,27]], children=[11]
- Black pixel id=11: boundary [[31, 28]]
So the green outline at cols 27-29 (id=8) contains the black pixel at [31,28] (id=11). And the green outline at cols 21-23 (id=6) contains child id=10.
Before:
- Green id=6: boundary [[30,18], [30,20], [32,20], [32,18]], children=[9] (white pixel)
- Green id=7: boundary [[30,27], [30,29], [32,29], [32,27]], children=[10] (black pixel)
So the green outline id=6 moved from cols 18-20 to cols 21-23 (right by 3). Its child changed from id=9 (white) to id=10 (black). The green outline id=7 at cols 27-29 is now id=8, and its child changed from id=10 (black) to id=11.
This is getting confusing with the IDs changing. Let me think about it visually:
Before:
- Left green box (white center) at cols 18-20
- Right green box (black center) at cols 27-29
- White block at cols 30-32
After:
- Left green box (white center) at cols 21-23  (moved right 3)
- White block at cols 24-26 (moved left 6? or it's a new position)
- Right green box (black center) at cols 27-29 (same position)
So the left green box moved right by 3, and the white block moved left by 6. They kind of swapped places.
Actually wait - looking at the ASCII more carefully:
Before row 30: ...wwwNNNwwwwwwNNNwww...
After row 30:  ...wwwwwNNNwwwNNNwww...
The gap between the two green boxes changed from 6 white cells to 3 white cells. The left green box moved right 3 cells, and the white block that was on the right side of the right green box moved to between the two green boxes.
Hmm, I think what happened is that the entire chain of objects (left green box, white block, right green box) shifted as a unit to the right by 3 cells. But the right green box stayed in place because it hit the purple bar.
Let me check: before, the right green box was at cols 27-29. The purple bar starts at col 33 (pppppp). After moving, the right green box is still at cols 27-29. So it couldn't move further right.
Actually, I think the RIGHT arrow key moves the leftmost green box (the one with the white center) to the right by 3 cells. When it moves, it pushes the white block with it, and the right green box stays in place because it's blocked by the purple bar.
Let me test this hypothesis by pressing RIGHT again and seeing what happens.
